A carefully constructed marker denoting a point of interest.
The Tomb of Peran and his Family. May his noble deed and word carry with him to the here after.

Bound in iron and with a heavy lock, this heavy chest was obviously meant to protect something of value.
Strange runes and pictographs, likely arcane in nature, have been scorched into the sides of this hide-covered strongbox.
Strange runes and pictographs, likely arcane in nature, have been scorched into the sides of this hide-covered strongbox.
Low and flat, this chest seems to have been designed for the storage and filing of a merchant's accounts.
Metals studs poke through the woodwork of this ornate chest. Judging by the fine craftsmanship, this strongbox appears to have been intended for public display.

Bastard Sword made out of adamantite.
Favored by rogues and rangers, this enchanted suit of elven leather armor provides added protection and increased stealth.
A suit of green and brown leather armor.

Suits of armor such as this are made using normal cotton, wool, and sometimes silk with padding made of soft leather strips.  The protection it offers is minimal, but it is extremely lightweight and produces little noise.
A suit of padded cloth armor.
This Darkwood shield is lighter than it's counter part

Ringmail is essentially the same as chainmail armor, larger links and a looser mesh make it lighter than chainmail with similar protection.  Ringmail however can be more easily pierced by missile weapons and long thin blades.  This suit created by dwarven smiths and is of exceptional quality.
